fields.brief_block: Caldwell v. State, 263 Ga. 560, 436 S.E.2d 488 (1993); see also Vega v. State, 285 Ga. 32, 673 S.E.2d 223 (2009) (holding it is “for the jury to determine the credibility of the witnesses and to resolve any conflicts or inconsistencies in the evidence”).
fields.finding_analysis: Vega v. State expressly states, in the course of rejecting the defendant's sufficiency challenge, that “It was for the jury to determine the credibility of the witnesses and to resolve any conflicts or inconsistencies in the evidence,” which matches the brief's quoted proposition verbatim. The principle appears as a quotation from Mickens v. State, but it is stated as the governing rule the Vega court applies, so the “see also” citation accurately supports the proposition.
